effect: Increased risk of TdP and/or QTc prolongation on ECG
mechanism: Concomitant blockade of cardiac potassium channels
management_strategies: Consider Alternatives: Alternative antimicrobials include penicillins and cephalosporins.

effect: Increased carbamazepine concentrations and risk of carbamazepine toxicity
mechanism: Inhibition of carbamazepine metabolism by CYP3A4
related_drugs: Azithromycin (Zithromax®) does not appear to inhibit CYP3A4
management_strategies: Consider Alternatives: Consider alternative antimicrobials (e.g., azithromycin, quinolones, 2nd/3rd generation cephalosporins, penicillin).
Monitor: Monitor carbamazepine concentrations and consider temporary downward adjustment of dosage (30-50%). Warn patients about symptoms of toxicity.

effect: Increased digoxin concentrations and risk of digoxin toxicity
mechanism: Inhibition of digoxin transport by P-glycoprotein resulting in reduced elimination
related_drugs: Isolated reports of digoxin toxicity associated with azithromycin (Zithromax®)
management_strategies: Consider Alternatives: Use antimicrobials that do not inhibit P-glycoprotein (e.g., cephalosporins, penicillin, quinolones).
Monitor: Evaluate for toxicity (nausea, visual changes, arrhythmias). Downward dosage adjustments may be needed.

effect: Increased digoxin concentrations and risk of digoxin toxicity
mechanism: Inhibition of digoxin transport by P-glycoprotein
related_drugs: Voriconazole (Vfend®) and terbinafine (Lamisil®) do not appear to inhibit P-glycoprotein
management_strategies: Consider Alternatives: Use voriconazole or terbinafine.
Monitor: Monitor closely for digoxin toxicity with downward dosage adjustments as necessary.

effect: Increased simvastatin concentrations and risk of myopathy/rhabdomyolysis
mechanism: Inhibition of simvastatin metabolism by CYP3A4
related_drugs: Atorvastatin (Lipitor®) has lower risk; Fluvastatin, Rosuvastatin, and Pravastatin are not metabolized by CYP3A4
management_strategies: Consider Alternatives: Statin alternatives include fluvastatin, rosuvastatin, or pravastatin.
Monitor: Simvastatin dose should not exceed 20 mg/day if used with amiodarone. Monitor for muscle pain or dark urine.

effect: Increased statin concentrations and risk of myopathy/rhabdomyolysis
mechanism: Inhibition of statin metabolism by CYP3A4
related_drugs: Fluvastatin, Rosuvastatin, Pravastatin are not metabolized by CYP3A4
management_strategies: Temporarily Hold Statin: Hold therapy during short-term azole use (e.g., 2 weeks).
Consider Alternatives: Use terbinafine or non-CYP3A4 statins.

mechanism: Inhibition of tamoxifen activation to its active metabolite by CYP2D6
related_drugs: Citalopram, Sertraline are weak inhibitors; Venlafaxine does not significantly inhibit CYP2D6
management_strategies: Consider Alternatives: Use citalopram, desvenlafaxine, escitalopram, fluvoxamine, sertraline, or venlafaxine.

effect: Increased warfarin concentrations and bleeding risk
mechanism: Inhibition of warfarin metabolism by CYP2C9
management_strategies: Monitor: Increase INR monitoring frequency. Dosage reduction of 30-50% is often required.

effect: Increased warfarin concentrations and bleeding risk
mechanism: Inhibition of warfarin metabolism by CYP2C9
related_drugs: Itraconazole, Ketoconazole, Terbinafine do not appear to inhibit CYP2C9
management_strategies: Consider Alternatives: Use Itraconazole, Ketoconazole, or Terbinafine.
Monitor: Carefully monitor INR if an interacting azole is started or stopped.

related_drugs: Barbiturates, Phenytoin, Primidone also reduce effect
management_strategies: Monitor: Monitor for altered warfarin response. Induction is gradual (several weeks).

effect: Increased warfarin effect and bleeding risk
related_drugs: Niacin is not known to interact
management_strategies: Consider Alternatives: Atorvastatin or Pravastatin are safer for lipid management.
Monitor: Monitor INR closely when starting or stopping fibrates.

mechanism: Antiplatelet effects and GI erosion from NSAIDs combined with anticoagulation
related_drugs: Acetaminophen (limit to 2g/day) or opioids are preferred
management_strategies: Consider Alternatives: Use acetaminophen or opioid analgesics.
Monitor: Monitor for GI bleeding signs if used together.

effect: Increased warfarin concentrations and bleeding risk
related_drugs: Atorvastatin and Pravastatin are not known to interact
management_strategies: Consider Alternatives: Use Atorvastatin or Pravastatin.
Monitor: Monitor INR when statins are started or changed.

effect: Increased warfarin concentrations and bleeding risk
mechanism: Inhibition of warfarin metabolism by CYP2C9
related_drugs: Penicillins and Cephalosporins are preferred alternatives
management_strategies: Consider Alternatives: Use penicillins or cephalosporins.
Monitor: Temporarily reduce warfarin dose and monitor INR closely.

mechanism: Accelerated depletion of vitamin K-dependent clotting factors
management_strategies: Monitor: Frequent INR monitoring (e.g., Day 4, Day 7, weekly) when thyroid therapy starts.
